speaker: Lynette
content: Boys! Guys, I'm begging you. Sit in your seats. I'm not gonna tell you again. Buckle up. I mean it, so help me... Oh, Crap!
mark: I'm begging you 我求求你
buckle up 系好安全带 buckle v. 发软；屈服；（使）弯曲；扣住 n. 搭扣
I mean it 我是认真的
so help me 我发誓；老天作证

speaker: Police
content: License and registration, please. Ma'am, you know why I pulled you over?
mark: License and registration 驾照和车辆登记证 License n. 执照，许可证；特许 registration n. 登记，注册，挂号；登记证，注册证；
Ma'am n. 女士；夫人，太太；小姐
pull over 靠边停车 pull you over 拦下你

speaker: Lynette
mark: I have a theory 我知道;我有一个推测 theory n.学说，理论

speaker: Police
content: The kids are jumping up and down, they should be sitting, wearing seat belts.
mark: jump up and down 上下跳跃
should be doing sth 应该正在做某事
seat belt 座椅安全带  belt n.腰带，皮带；传送带；地带
wear seat belt 戴安全带

speaker: Lynette
content: I yelled at them. They never listen to me. It's very frustrating.
mark: yell at 大声责骂，呵斥 yell v.叫喊，大喊
frustrate  v. 使懊恼，使沮丧；挫败，阻挠；受挫

speaker: Police
content: You’re gonna hafta find a way to control them. After all, that's your job.
mark: hafta <主美口>=have to
After all 毕竟

speaker: Alice
content: Though he'd been a policeman for six years, officer Hayes had never found himself in a truly dangerous situation.
mark: policeman n. 警察，警员
officer n. 军官；官员，高级职员；警察，警官；船长；

speaker: Alice
content: Then again, he had never before told a woman how to raise her children.
mark: Then again 另一方面；话说回来
raise v. 抚养，养育；提起，举起；饲养，种植；

speaker: Lynette
content: Are you saying I'm a bad mother?

speaker: Police
content: Ma’am, you need to get back in your car please.

speaker: Lynette
content: I have no help. My husband's always away on business.
mark: be away on business 因公出差；因公务外出

speaker: Police
content: I'm gonna have to ask you to step back now.
mark: I’m gonna have to do sth 我将不得不做某事
step back 退后

speaker: Lynette
content: My babysitter joined the witness relocation program.
mark: babysitter n. 临时保姆
witness relocation program 证人安置计划 witness 目击者；证人；relocation n. 重新安置；再布置；变换布置 program n. 程序；计划，规划；

speaker: Lynette
content: I haven't slept through the night in six years and for you to stand there and judge me.
mark: through the night 整夜地，通宵地
... and for you to do sth 作为句子的一部分，通常用于表达目的、结果或进一步的行为等，和前面的内容有逻辑上的承接关系。
judge v. 判断，认为；裁判，评判；估计 n. 法官，审判官；裁判员；鉴定人

speaker: Police
content: OK. I'm not gonna give you a ticket. I'm just gonna let you off with a warning.
mark: give you a ticket 给你开一张罚单；给你一张票
let sb off with sth 对某人从轻处罚；放过某人

speaker: Lynette
mark: apology n. 道歉
